content.problemStatement: Write a function named **calculatePower** that takes two integer, **base** and **exponent** respectively, and returns the result of raising **base** to the power of **exponent**
constraints: All results fit into a signed 64‑bit integer
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains two space‑separated integers: base and exponent.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output a single line containing base raised to the power exponent.

sampleTestCases.explanation: There is one test case. 2 raised to the power 3 equals 8.
Any number to the power 0 is defined as 1.

solution.approach: Use binary (fast) exponentiation. Initialise result = 1. While exponent > 0, if the current exponent bit is 1 multiply result by the current base. Square the base and shift exponent right. This runs in O(log exponent) time and O(1) extra space. Repeat for each test case.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T * log exponent) – each test case needs at most 30 iterations because exponent ≤ 30.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a few integer variables are used.
